steps.title: Identify the temporal signal
steps.content: ”When she made an interesting discovery” marks a specific moment in the past with the simple past verb “made.”

steps.title: Choose the correct past tense
steps.content:
An action that was ongoing when that discovery happened requires the past progressive—”was studying” indicates the data study was in progress at the moment of discovery. The remaining choices use present tense (studies), future tense (will study), or present perfect (has been studying), none of which fit a specific past moment when something else occurred.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
